title: Level 116

__NOTOC__{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 116: "Peaceful Pens"}}{{M.E.G. Theme}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Overpass:ital,wght@0,100;0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;0,900;1,100;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800;1,900&display=swap'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Overpass', sans-serif; } }} {{CSS|content=a { font-family: Overpass; }}
{{Quote|'''Level 116''', one of the safest levels one can come across in the Backrooms. Its safety might even reach the likes of [[Level 330]] and [[Level 399]]. Sure, it isn't luxurious, but it is still infinitely better than most levels.|Unknown, February 12, 2002}} {{Background|file=386rev1img1.jpg}}
---- {{Image Banner|image=386rev1img2.jpg|caption=
The cozy sheds await your arrival.
}} {{Alt Class|no=116|ext=2|env=1|ent=1}}
Level 116 is the 117th level of the Backrooms.
General Description
General Description
{{Blurimage|caption=A high-quality image of '''Level 116'''.|file=386rev1img1.jpg|hovertext=Brand New Beginnings}} {{Blurimage|caption=Another image of '''Level 116'''.|file=386rev1img2.jpg|hovertext=It is Safe... for now...}} '''Level 116''' is an extensive livestock farming complex. It is unclear what the complex's exact original intended purpose is, as areas differ in livestock type, mainly pigs, cows, horses, and chickens. The level situates itself in a tropical environment, featuring tropical plants, a humid atmosphere, and moderately high temperatures. '''Level 116''' emphasizes a day-and-night cycle, with day lasting 23 hours and night lasting 4 hours, totaling 27 hours for a complete cycle. The level has no related livestock or animal life and is home to a few passive entities. Most of the expansive complex comprises regular livestock sheds and barns. One can seldom stumble upon other unusual places within the complex, such as underground bunkers, engine rooms, nipa huts, canals, empty restaurants, swimming pools, and car garages. These out-of-place structures are well-spaced out from each other to provide continuity and consistency with the level's theme. These random structures contain many valuable objects for the wanderers to use. Edible [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] abundantly line the corners and crevices of '''Level 116.''' These Backshrooms regrow quickly after being plucked, have a faster growth and spread rate than regular Backshrooms, and can grow on any surface. Occasionally, one may find animal feeding containers filled partially with clean [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. Almond Water also exists on '''Level 116''' in bottle form and is unusually slightly greater in volume than regular Almond Water bottles. Like [[Level 1]], supply crates often pop up randomly throughout '''Level 116'''. They sporadically materialize as long as wanderers are not looking and if there are no other supply crates nearby. They can contain assortments of different objects, usually agriculturally themed, such as watering cans, bundles of hay, fertilizer bags, etc. Vital items include [[Object 1|Almond Water]], sanity pills, processed [[Object 10|Backshroom]] foodstuffs, and chiselsOne can utilize chisels in the gathering of [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] on '''Level 116'''.. These supply crates only appear as wooden and cannot be found comprised of any other material, presumably due to the farming theme of '''Level 116'''. Similarly to [[Level 399]], structural damage in '''Level 116''' heals over time, depending on its severity. Light damage regenerates quicker, whereas heavier damage can potentially take months to heal. When an area sustains a certain level of damage, it can no longer heal itself without human intervention. Rains and monsoons are common weather phenomena in '''Level 116'''. When this occurs, it boosts [[Object 10|Backshroom]] growth, produces gusts of cool air to balance the overall temperature, and produces gushing water to cleanse grime, grit, and dust. Once the rain stops, any remaining excess water rapidly evaporates, and in 15 minutes, most areas will dry up as if it had never rained. Rainwater is unsafe to consume without some filtering processes. '''Level 116''' posits a distinct, farm-like, putrid scent. This scent manifests itself outside of rain events and specific randomized locations. One can mitigate smelling this odor by drinking [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or consuming specific species of [[Object 10|Backshrooms]], such as cinnamon and flesh shrooms.
Entities
Entities
[[Kats]] are the only entity roaming '''Level 116'''. Other unconfirmed entity sightings include [[Entity 36|Lighters]], [[Entity 34|Blood Wasps]], [[Entity 83|Cone-Spiders]], and [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]. Note that these accounts are from wanderers who have lost most of their sanity and reported that the entities had vanished after sipping on an instance of [[Object 1|Almond Water]].
Settlements, Colonies, and Outposts
Settlements, Colonies, and Outposts
Currently, no established communities or outposts exist within '''Level 116'''. However, multiple groups have plans to create communities or outposts within the level.
Entrances & Exits
Entrances & Exits
===
Entrances
=== *Entering a wooden door in [[Level 23]] leads wanderers here. *Noclipping through a stack of hay in [[Level 67]] sends one here. ===
Exits
=== *Noclipping through a black floor sends one to [[Level 25]]. *Yellow doors send one to [[Level 0]]. *Noclipping through the bottom of a swimming pool sends one to [[Level 102]]. *Trapdoors with crystals growing on it send one to [[Level 500]]. {{Footnotes}} ---- ----

'''You got mail!'''
'''16/5/2011'''
----- '''From:''' Sergeant Ella (isabelladreyfus@backmail.com)
'''To:''' Fabius Solsbury (FabsolMEG@backmail.com)
'''Subject:''' You have obtained access to new classified documents. -----
Hello Fabius! This is me, Sergeant Isabella, although you can simply refer to me as Ella. Since you have now been promoted to a higher position, you now have access to medium-level classified documents. However, there was an error with the database when allocating new classified information so that you are able to access it, so we at the Major Explorer Group figured that we manually send all the classififed information to you via email. Such a tedious process!

This email regards '''Level 116''', more specifically a brand new revision of it alongside a copius amount of logs. I personally don't know too much about this document, so I suppose you read it all yourself, if you want to, that is.

You know the drill at this point, '''keep all classified documents to yourself and do not share with ANYONE outside of [[M.E.G.]] authorized personnel, not even family and friends.''' Violating said rule will lead to a severe punishment depending on the document's classification level.
- Isabella

Access Granted!
Welcome to the Major Explorer Group Database, Fabius. ----
[[File:M.E.G without the text.png|center|150px]] '''''September 21, 2003'''''

[[File:116logimg1.jpg|400px|link=]]
Alright. We start off with humble beginnings. It is just how any normal M.E.G. settlement, or almost any planned settlement, begins. We are provided a few rations, then set up communications with the main base and find a proper location to establish the settlement, bla bla bla. So far, it has been a few days since this operation first began, and it is all going well with nearly zero issues, even minor nuisances. We have already created a proper food and water supply, found a reasonable place to establish the colony, and, conveniently enough, a sizeable laboratory area right next to our planned building area, in which we can further research and gather more information about our findings. We still have more to go before we begin the actual operation, once the requirements have been met. I hope this goes very well and that we discover new information while we create a thriving society in '''Level 116'''. A win-win situation, one might even say! God bless the Major Explorer Group and the Backrooms as a whole!

[[File:M.E.G without the text.png|center|150px]] '''''January 8, 2004'''''

We met the initial criteria to fully begin the operation. We are making so much food that we have to throw some of it out. Water, too, isn't a big issue, although it is hard to come by sometimes. Our population is rapidly increasing, with an estimated new person every day! All of that comes together now, and in the next few days, the full power of the operation will begin. I, along with everyone else, am extremely nervous about the operation and if it will even go well; after all, this is the Backrooms, almost nothing truly goes well.

But what is this operation about?



For the longest time, many have wondered the true biological nature of entities in the Backrooms. While we have made some progress, dissecting the biology of an entity is easier said than done. This operation, and everything that we are doing in '''Level 116''''s main point revolve around studying more about the Backrooms' anomalous entities. We plan to do this by using the pre-existing infrastructure of '''Level 116''' to house our test subjects. We plan to first actually house them, study them more, and even learn how to breed them. This is going to be a very tedious operation, but as with how well everything is going so far, I am sure it will go very well.

[[File:M.E.G without the text.png|center|150px]] '''''September 19, 2004'''''

[[File:116logimg2.jpg|300px|link=]]
Development has now been finished on a new serum that will calm any hostile entity, with few exceptions! This was three years in the making and was a collaboration effort with [[Z.E.T.A.]] and the B.E.O.. This is a marvel of biological engineering for the Backrooms as a whole. One doesn't have to inject the entity with the serum in order to neutralize it, they can just spray the serum on the entity for its effects to work. Additionally, the serum doesn't pollute the environment, it does not release any harmful gases even when it is being produced or accidentally spilled into the surrounding environs; it almost sounds too good to be true. Now, we just have to mass produce this serum, which is going to be easy since [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] comprise most of its ingredients.

[[File:M.E.G without the text.png|center|150px]] '''''May 30, 2005'''''

[[File:116logimg3.jpg|350px|link=]]
The new [[Jantlers]] are loving their new home, aren't they? Three years in the program, preparations included, it is all going well. We are climbing up new heights every time. The bigger the colony grows, the more test subjects we have, the more knowledge we research and gather, and the more technological innovations we make; it is an unending upward trend. We are going through some small issues, but these issues are mainly minor nuisances which get resolved that by the next day it seems as if nothing ever happened. We also have children moving into the colony and some have even produced offspring in '''Level 116'''! ''For three years, nothing of real interest was happening. The colony members posted the same logs about making new advancements and new expansions, etc. These were all great news to the wanderers residing in the Backrooms, as technology pushes forward and more knowledge has been gathered. However, the last log was posted below on '''May 13, 2008''', the last known log in the database.''

[[File:M.E.G without the text.png|center|150px]] '''''May 13, 2008'''''

[[File:116logimg4.jpg|350px|link=]]
Alright... I don't even know where to start...

The past night has been horrible, I must say. The strongest recorded rainstorm in '''Level 116''' decimated our colony, sustaining minor yet significant damages. Many important assets are now destroyed, either because they are malfunctioning, heavily damaged, or straight up lost. The aftermath of this unforgiving tempest also flooded the level, which created a myriad of issues for the next week. But that's not the worst. Our entity docility serum sprinkler broke down from last night's storm, and now we can't have the serum properly distributed throughout our massive colony of test subjects, and now '''the worst is yet to come''', as the serum will wear off, and the ungodly wrath of the entities will unleash upon all of us. The storm also destroyed all of our serum tanks, so we are left with almost no usable serum.

The end is near for us, we must accept that this is the end... '''This was the last recorded log in the database.'''
'''On ''May 16, 2011'', a group of three [[M.E.G.]] expeditioners, namely Christine, Arthur, and Eric. Their objective was to explore the level and gather as much data as they can.'''
'''May 16, 2011''' '''Eric:''' Alright teammates. Make sure to always follow my lead and do not veer off too far away from me.

'''Christine:''' and '''Arthur:''' Yes, sir.

'''Eric:''' So this is it, a way to enter '''Level 116'''.

'''Christine:''' A rotten wooden door, in [[Level 23]].

'''Arthur:''' Are you sure we are prepared?

'''Eric:''' Yes, we are prepared. Each of us are carrying a few MREs, three cans of almond water, and a walkie-talkie to communicate back to main base.

'''Christine:''' We are good to go. Now, let's open that wooden door!



[[File:116logimg.webp|420px|link=]]
'''Eric:''' This place...

'''Everyone in unison:''' is decimated.

'''Arthur:''' I have almost no words.

'''Eric:''' Me neither. To think this was once a bustling science city just three years back is... surreal to say the least.

'''Arthur:''' Just being here already makes me feel sick to my stomach.

'''Eric:''' That last log in the database hinted at an extremely catastrophic event that occurred to this once serene level.

'''Christine:''' I see destruction everywhere. Dilapidating structural integrity, decaying corpses, overgrowth, a foul stencch, and the absence of resources, just to name a few...

'''Eric:''' Alright, we should keep exploring.


'''Arthur:'''This bar... I remember this bar. I remember my colleague always hanging out here. Now it's just a dilapidated mess, filled with corpses, rotten drinks, mysterious objects and substances, slightly submerged in musty water.

'''Christine:''' The following events that have transpired are getting more and more obvious.

'''Eric:''' Seems like, shortly after the rainstorm the entities broke free, unleasing a relentless bloodlust on the level, killing everyone who could not escape.

'''Christine:''' Well I guess no one was able to escape, no one lived to tell the story.

'''Arthur:''' We may as well be the first ones to do so...

'''Christine:''' Wait... WAIT... If the entities broke free, killed every member of the colony, they could just be roaming around right now, hiding in plain sight, waiting for their next meal...

'''Everyone in unison:''' Well... shit.

'''Eric:''' We must practice extreme caution here to not awaken any devilish entities.

'''Christine:''' It just gets worse, the more we stay, the more dread we experience...

'''Eric:''' I recommend you talk in a low voice, to not awaken the entities.

'''Arthur:''' I feel like we should have encountered at least one by now. I guess we are lucky.

'''Christine:''' Everything here is just making me feel more uneasy...

'''Arthur:''' All three of us can say the same thing.

'''Christine:''' I feel like I'm starting to hallucinate. I saw something that my mind thought was a [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealer]] and I nearly screamed at the top of my lungs.

'''Eric:''' The situation just worsens every passing second.

'''Christine:''' Wait, we have our walkie-talkies, right?

'''Eric:''' Yeah, why?

'''Christine:''' I feel like now is high time to report our findings to the headquarters, and maybe even say our final goodbyes to our loved ones.

'''Eric:''' If we even had any loved ones in the Backrooms, that is. But it makes sense, some people really matter to you.

'''Arthur:''' I spot some firearms around here. We could use these when battling entities.

'''Eric:''' That's a horrible idea, the firearm could easily attract a horde of entities. Plus, how are you even going to get ammunition, and see if it is even compatible with your gun?

'''Arthur:''' Fair enough.

'''Eric:''' Christine is right. We should report our findings to the headquarters right now, in the dire case we don't make it out alive.

'''Eric:''' Wait. Hold up...

'''Eric:''' The walkie talkies FAIL TO BOOT UP! THEY DON'T EVEN WORK!.

'''''A distant screeching noise reverberates throughout the dilapidated barns...'''''
 '''Christine:''' You... You... startled a [[Entity 8|Hound]]! I'm not even joking!!

'''Eric:''' I know! Everyone, Run in the opposite direction as fast as you can!...

'''Arthur:''' Shit, I wasn't prepared mentally for this..

'''Christine:''' Okay Arthur, that doesn't matter, just RUN!

'''''The hound caught up to Arthur, brutally mutilating him. Eric runs as fast as he can, but tripped and noclipped through a puddle. Christine, the last person in the pack, ran as fast as she can and luckily encountered a musty swimming pool, in which she jumped in and safely entered [[Level 330]].

However, after she entered the blissful level, she sat down in a lounge chair, still comprehending the events that transpired in the last fifteen minutes, lamenting joining the expedition...'''''
{{Blurquote|image=116logimg.webp|quote-text=I am now here, in a [[Level 330|way safer level]], but that doesn't stop my anxiety from taking over. I still fail to wrap my head around what just happened an hour ago. In a minute, I saw my closest colleagues perish horrifyingly, and all I could do was just run, hoping I don't have the same bloody fate. What occurred from that once short incident will scar me for the rest of time. The merciless growl of that [[Entity 8|hound]], the screams of Eric, my heart beating very fast, whilst panic was coarsing through my veins... I will have sleepless nights for months, horrific flashbacks for years, scarring images for eternity; my mind has been tainted to an incomprehensible extent. All I can just do now is stay here, enjoy the safety of this level, retire from the [[M.E.G.]] and join [[Level 399]]'s Megacolony... I can hide the pain with that much bliss, but the mental scars will never be truly healed.|person=Christine}}
'''''Ever since that incident, the Major Explorer Group decided to only use drones and similar technology to explore Level 116.'''''
---- ---- ---- ---- {{Quote|Oftentimes, experiments can go horribly wrong, for lack of a better word. Especially in a demented hellscape such as the Backrooms.|Unknown, September 28, 2022}}
---- {{Image Banner|image=116truevers1.jpg|caption=
You feel an evil presence watching you...
}} {{Alt Class|no=116|ext=4|env=4|ent=5}}
Level 116 is the 117th level of the Backrooms.
Description
Description
{{Blurimage|caption=A recent image of '''Level 116'''.|file=116truevers1.webp|hovertext=Manmade Hellscape}} {{Blurimage|caption=Another image of '''Level 116'''.|file=116truevers1.jpg|hovertext=Forbidden Farms}} Previously a haven, '''Level 116''' is a heavily deteriorated livestock farming complex. The level consists of rotten wooden planks, waterlogged foundations, overgrowth, and many other signs of unmaintained dilapidation. Water constantly drips from the ceilings; all paneling is prone to collapsing; wooden planks always make creaking noises when stepped on; Hydrolitis Plague permeates stagnant waters; the sky is usually blanketed in gloomy clouds, producing a slight drizzle; and several entities lurk around the corner, hidden in plain sight. '''Level 116''''s only light source is a celestial body looming above the sky behind the dark clouds, providing substantial light. The level has a day and night cycle; the duration of night and day constantly varies. '''Level 116''' was once a bustling haven, housing thousands of wanderers until disaster struck. Before this catastrophic event, Level 116 had no hostile entities and had several valuable items such as supply crates, [[Object 1|Almond Water]], and [[Object 10|Backshrooms]]. Nowadays, supply crates are nowhere to be seen. [[Object 44|Cashew Water]] replaces all known instances of Almond Water, and all Backshroom variants growing in '''Level 116''' are volatile and are thus unsafe to consume. Only one major colony existed before the occurrence of this apocalyptic event. Many blame the catastrophic event on the actions of this colony, spiraling out of control and wreaking havoc on what was once a safe getaway. This colony was once a thriving [[M.E.G.]] base that utilized the level for a testing facility centered around entities. One day, an unforeseen event ravaged the colony, releasing all the captive entities free and wreaking havoc on the idyllic level. Ever since that moment, the M.E.G. has gathered information about the current state of '''Level 116''' via unmanned means, such as drones. The level's nature changed drastically since that event, as its weather intensified dramatically, its structural integrity degraded quickly, and entrances and exits now vary and fluctuate constantly, among many other changes. The level posits an interlevel communication phenomenon. Most attempts to transmit information from '''Level 116''' fail frequently, and in rare instances where an information transmission attempt succeeds, some information will become corrupted. This anomaly hinders many expeditions to gather information about the level, and thus, the Major Explorer Group has difficulty gathering information regarding Level 116.
Entities
Entities
Hostile entities roam the dilapidated complex of '''Level 116''', mainly [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], Female [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], the [[Hydrolitis Plague]], [[Entity 22|Anethikas]], and [[Entity 34|Blood Wasps]]. '''Level 116''' is the highest-numbered level home to [[Entity 22|Anethikas]].
Settlements, Colonies, and Outposts
Settlements, Colonies, and Outposts
As previously mentioned, '''Level 116''' was once home to a thriving [[M.E.G.]] colony until the calamitous event. The current nature of the level renders itself strictly uninhabitable for wanderers.
Entrances and Exits
Entrances and Exits
{{Blurimage|caption=An image of an exit to [[Level 0]], one of the only guaranteed methods to exit '''Level 116'''.|file=116trueversexit.jpg|hovertext=Finally, the End.|width=195px|font-size=82%}} Note that '''Level 116''''s entrances and exits fluctuate randomly during time intervals. Listed below are the level's guaranteed entrances and exits, which many experts theorize will remain within the level perpetually. ===
Entrances
=== *Entering a wooden door in [[Level 23]] leads wanderers here. ===
Exits
=== *Noclipping through a black floor sends one to [[Level 25]]. *Yellow doors send one to [[Level 0]]. *Noclipping through the bottom of a swimming pool sends one to [[Level 102]]. *Trapdoors with crystals growing on it send one to [[Level 500]].
